raw_text.response: Let me state the question here. There are four people, Doris, Bob, Jack, and Annie. They need to cross the bridge, but it's dark outside, and they only have one flashlight. Doris takes 10 minutes, Bob takes 5 minutes, Jack takes 2 minutes, and Annie takes 1 minute to cross the bridge. Only two people can cross at a time, and someone must return with a flashlight after each crossing. How can they get across in 17 minutes? Okay, that's a very interesting problem. Because if you add all the time, it's already 18 minutes and somebody has to return. Okay, let me think. So... So basically, let me think for a moment. So if Doris and Annie goes and Annie returns, Bob and Annie goes and Annie returns, Jack and Annie go together, it already takes 10 plus 1 plus 5 plus 1 plus 2 equals... say, 19 minutes, right? That already exceeds the 17 minutes limit given in the problem. So let me think for a moment, right? OK. I think it's very important for Doris and Bob to get across together because like there are some like more fast people they can like come and go multiple times but if Doris and we can overlap the time of Doris and Bob it will indeed speed up the process right so first let me think of this first Jack and Annie crosses together it takes two minutes and Annie comes back and we used three minutes in total. And using the flashlight that Annie has brought back, Doris and Bob crosses together. It will take 10 minutes and so it's 13 minutes and Jack's come back, it's 15 minutes and Jack and Annie crosses back, it's 17 minutes. So we solved the problem, so the solution is Annie and Jack cross together, Annie comes back, Doris and Bob cross together, Jack comes back and Jack and Annie crosses together and that's the finish.
